metadata.title: Why "Maybe We Should" Becomes "Decision: We Will"
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Claude Cowork candidate 02. Source chapter: claude-cowork/chapters/09-research-packets-and-meeting-notes.md. One mechanism: language models smooth toward coherence and decisiveness, quietly upgrading the modality of statements without inventing anything.
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL #1F6F5C = original tentative note / faithful record; CRIMSON #BF3339 = upgraded/decided version; GOLD = editor's-pen highlight fill only, once
metadata.note: Exclusions: NO meeting-summary column template, NO contextual-integrity privacy theory, NO four-operations taxonomy, NO second artifact type, NO ICMJE authorship discussion.

beats.narration_text: Tuesday's team meeting. A few things agreed, a few tabled. You hand the notes to Cowork. The summary is polished and wrong.

props.headline: Tuesday's team meeting. A few things agreed, a few tabled. You…

card.copy: Why "Maybe We Should" Becomes "Decision: We Will"

beats.narration_text: Because Cowork did not invent anything exactly. Every word is somewhere in the spirit of those notes. The spirit is not the record.

props.headline: Because Cowork did not invent anything exactly. Every word is somewhere…

beats.scene_description: rough handwritten meeting notes on a notepad — imprecise, with crossings-out, tentative phrases
beats.image_prompt: B02, close-up of rough handwritten meeting notes on a notepad, imprecise scribbly text, crossed-out words, tentative phrases visible, editorial collage style, desaturated newsprint treatment, warm cream tones, no faces

beats.narration_text: Here is the question. A faithful summary should preserve 'maybe we should look into this.' The summary reads 'Decision: we will.' Nothing was fabricated. Why?

props.headline: Here is the question. A faithful summary should preserve 'maybe we…

document.quote: A faithful summary should preserve 'maybe we should look into this.' The summary reads 'Decision: we will.' Nothing was fabricated. Why?

beats.narration_text: Here is the original note: 'silent auction — tabled, needs board input.' This is a statement about modality. About how decided a thing is.

props.headline: Here is the original note: 'silent auction — tabled, needs board…

viz.label: original note in TEAL: 'silent auction — tabled, needs board input'

beats.narration_text: Here is the summary output: 'Decision: add a silent auction.' The statement has been promoted. From tentative to committed. Nothing was added. The certainty was.

props.headline: Here is the summary output: 'Decision: add a silent auction.' The…

viz.label: summary in CRIMSON below original: 'DECISION: add a silent auction'

beats.narration_text: Language models optimize for coherent, resolved prose. That is what the pattern produces. A discussion sounds like an agreement because agreement sounds more resolved.

props.headline: Language models optimize for coherent, resolved prose. That is what the…

viz.label: two text styles: hedged vs resolved; CRIMSON arrow shows model pull toward resolved

beats.narration_text: The model quietly upgrades modality. 'Maybe we should look into this' becomes 'agreed.' 'It might be worth discussing' becomes 'action item.' Hedges are removed. Certainty is implied.

props.headline: The model quietly upgrades modality. 'Maybe we should look into this'…

viz.label: modality spectrum: discussed -> maybe -> agreed -> decided; CRIMSON arrow shows model upgrade

beats.narration_text: This is the mechanism: the model produces what prose looks like when decisions were made. It does not know what actually happened in the room.

props.headline: This is the mechanism: the model produces what prose looks like…

document.quote: The model produces what prose looks like when decisions were made.

beats.narration_text: Someone who only asked a question now has a task assigned. No one gave them the task. Coherent prose did.

props.headline: Someone who only asked a question now has a task assigned.…

viz.label: TEAL: 'who owns the venue contract?' -> CRIMSON: '[Name] — follow-up task'
viz.note: second case: question becomes action item

beats.narration_text: The artifact lands in the team's inbox. It looks like the official record. Three people push back. One person is confused about a task attributed to them.

props.headline: The artifact lands in the team's inbox. It looks like the…

beats.scene_description: a polished meeting summary printout sitting in an office inbox, looking official and decisive
beats.image_prompt: B11, close-up of a clean formatted meeting summary document in an office inbox tray, bold headers and bulleted action items, official looking, editorial collage style, desaturated newsprint treatment, cream and charcoal tones, no face

beats.narration_text: Illustrative. A 45-minute call. The note: 'silent auction — tabled, needs board input.' The summary Decisions section: 'Agreed: add a silent auction.' The tabled item became a commitment.

props.headline: Illustrative. A 45-minute call. The note: 'silent auction — tabled, needs…

viz.label: source note (TEAL) vs Decisions row (CRIMSON) side by side; 'illustrative' label

beats.narration_text: And the attendee who asked 'who owns the venue contract?' is listed in Action Items as owning a follow-up no one assigned them.

props.headline: And the attendee who asked 'who owns the venue contract?' is…

viz.label: TEAL question -> CRIMSON action item with owner assigned; 'illustrative'

beats.narration_text: Discussed is not agreed. The summary does not know the difference. You do.

props.headline: Discussed is not agreed. The summary does not know the difference.…

beats.name: OutroSeries
beats.narration_text: Part of the Claude Cowork series.
